_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The IMO 2023 Revised GHG Strategy is a framework instrument adopted unanimously by 175 IMO Member States at MEPC 80 on 7 July 2023. It is not itself legally binding but establishes the ambition and timeline against which binding MARPOL Annex VI amendments are developed. The currently in-force legally binding measures implementing the strategy are the Energy Efficiency Existing Ship Index (EEXI) and Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII), both mandatory from 1 January 2023 under MARPOL Annex VI amendments adopted in 2021. The next layer — the IMO Net-Zero Framework (NZF) — was approved in principle at MEPC 83 in April 2025 and awaits formal adoption, expected around October 2026 with entry into force approximately 16 months thereafter.

In the supply-chain compliance taxonomy, obligations under the current MARPOL Annex VI regime fall on **shipowners and operators** (flag-state responsibility) and, to a lesser degree, on **charterers** through voyage charter clauses affecting vessel selection and speed. **Freight forwarders** — including the workspace — occupy the position of **cargo interest / beneficial cargo owner (BCO) agent** in this taxonomy. This means the workspace is not a directly regulated entity under MARPOL Annex VI or the 2023 GHG Strategy; however, it faces three vectors of indirect exposure:

1. **Cost pass-through**: Compliance costs borne by carriers (CII corrective actions, future NZF Remedial Unit purchases) will be passed to shippers and, by extension, to the workspace's clients via bunker adjustment factors or new GHG surcharges.
2. **Scope 3 reporting obligations**: Clients in the workspace's luxury, automotive, and film/TV verticals increasingly face mandatory or voluntary Scope 3 Category 4/9 disclosure (e.g., under CSRD, ISSB S2, or SEC climate rules). The workspace, as the arranger of transportation services, will be required to provide verified well-to-wake emission data per shipment.
3. **Carrier qualification**: As the NZF and CII regimes tighten, vessel CII ratings and GFI attainment will become material criteria for carrier selection, equivalent to capacity or transit-time considerations today.

Role placement requiring legal confirmation: Whether the workspace, when acting as a contractual carrier (issuing its own HBL/HAWB under NVOCC authority), inherits any charter-party-level GHG compliance obligations through back-to-back contracts with ocean carriers requires jurisdiction-specific legal review, particularly for US-flagged trade lanes where US maritime law interacts with IMO frameworks differently from EU or UK law.

*Source: IMO Press Briefing — Revised GHG Reduction Strategy for Global Shipping Adopted, IMO, 7 July 2023. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/revised-ghg-reduction-strategy-for-global-shipping-adopted-.aspx; IMO MEPC 80 Meeting Summary, IMO, July 2023. https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/meetingsummaries/pages/mepc-80.aspx*
